name: Thoth
shortDescription: Thoth is the ancient Egyptian god of writing, wisdom, and magic. He is often depicted with the head of an ibis or a baboon and is associated with the moon.
description: Thoth was a central figure in the Egyptian pantheon, revered as the god of wisdom, writing, hieroglyphs, science, magic, judgment, and the moon. He was believed to be the scribe of the gods, responsible for recording events and maintaining cosmic order. Thoth played a crucial role in the weighing of the heart ceremony in the afterlife, recording the results that determined whether a soul was worthy of eternal life. His knowledge was vast, encompassing all aspects of learning and intellectual pursuits. As the inventor of writing, he was the patron of scribes and libraries, ensuring the preservation of knowledge for future generations. He was also associated with the moon due to its cyclical nature and its role in measuring time. Thoth's influence extended to law, governance, and diplomacy, as his wisdom was sought in resolving disputes and maintaining justice. He was considered a wise counselor and a powerful magician, capable of manipulating the forces of nature. His symbol, the ibis, represented his wisdom and knowledge, while the baboon, another of his animal forms, symbolized his intelligence and wit. Thoth was a vital deity in Egyptian mythology, embodying the power of knowledge and the importance of order and balance in the universe. His association with the Eye of Horus through its restoration further solidifies his role as a healer and preserver.

sources.title: The Egyptian Book of the Dead
